key_insight: A weakly supervised PET representation-learning framework that uses H&E-derived pathology during training can improve retrieval of intra-tumour heterogeneity while still allowing PET-only inference at test time.
clinical_relevance: This could help build more informative PET-based tools for tumour characterization and case retrieval, potentially supporting precision oncology workflows without requiring pathology at inference.
method: Teacher–student training to learn PET tumour voxel representations from H&E-informed supervision.
Generate both global and hotspot-conditioned embeddings to capture informative tumour subregions.
Assess ablations, retrieval metrics, hotspot faithfulness, and prototype–histology concordance.
evidence_signal: Across cross-validation, progressive addition of pathology-informed supervision and hotspot modelling improved retrieval performance on mean average precision, nDCG, and MRR versus global PET and conventional PET baselines.
watch_item: The approach was evaluated in a specific oesophageal cancer test case, so broader generalization and practical deployment need validation on larger, diverse cohorts.
paper_title: Weakly Supervised Pathology-Informed Representation Learning for PET-Based Content Retrieval of Intra-Tumour Heterogeneity
